title: Kid-Friendly Chicken Fried Rice
description: A quick and easy one-pan chicken fried rice recipe that's perfect for a family dinner. It's packed with flavor and uses simple ingredients your kids will love.
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger, and stir-fry for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Push garlic and ginger to one side, crack eggs into the other side, and scramble until cooked through. Break into small pieces.
Add cooked chicken and frozen mixed vegetables to the skillet. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add day-old rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps. Pour in soy sauce and sesame oil. Stir well to combine and coat all ingredients.
Continue to stir-fry for another 5-7 minutes, ensuring the rice is heated through and slightly crispy.
Stir in chopped green onions just before serving.
tips: Using day-old rice is crucial for fried rice, as fresh rice is too moist and will result in a mushy texture.
Adjust soy sauce to taste; for younger children, you might use a low-sodium option or slightly less.
Feel free to add other kid-friendly vegetables like corn or peas if your family prefers them.
